_comment: Project-level MCP server config. Each entry adds a tool surface I can call directly. Most need an env var with a token (set in .env or in your shell). The 'enabled' field is read by Claude Code; flip to false to disable temporarily without deleting the entry.

github._setup: 1) Generate a fine-scoped GitHub PAT at https://github.com/settings/tokens (scopes: repo, read:org, workflow). 2) Add to your shell: export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_… or save to .env. 3) Restart Claude Code. I'll then be able to read issues, PR comments, action runs, and dispatch workflows.

supabase._setup: Already in your .env — reads SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY directly. After enabling I can run direct SQL against the production DB (read-only by default if you scope the key). Highest-leverage MCP for debugging customer issues.

postgres._setup: Alternative to Supabase MCP if you want raw Postgres access (e.g. to a Railway-hosted db). Pulls DATABASE_URL from .env. Use the pooled connection (the one with pgbouncer=true) so you don't exhaust direct connections.

filesystem._setup: Built-in already — Claude Code's Read/Edit/Write tools cover this. Listed for reference; can be removed if you prefer not to expose the second mount.

sentry._setup: 1) https://sentry.io/settings/account/api/auth-tokens/ — create a token with project:read, event:read scopes. 2) Set SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN + SENTRY_ORG (your Sentry org slug) in .env. 3) After enabling I can pull recent error events, group them, and proactively fix what's noisy. Sentry SDK is already wired in apps/api/src/sentry.ts.

railway._setup: 1) Generate at https://railway.app/account/tokens — 'Personal' scope is fine. 2) Add RAILWAY_API_TOKEN to .env. 3) After enabling I can list deployments, restart services, tail logs without you copy-pasting from the dashboard.
